risk_if_not_implemented: Without Use of Demilitarized Zones (DMZ), residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Boundary Protection
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to monitor and control communications at the external network boundary and at key internal boundaries within the network.
compensating_control_1.justification: Boundary Protection (NET-03) provides network-level access restriction that compensates for the absence of Use of Demilitarized Zones (DMZ) (WEB-02) by limiting attacker reach and lateral movement opportunities across the environment. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Network Segmentation (macrosegementation)
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to ensure network architecture utilizes network segmentation to isolate Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS) to protect from other network resources.
compensating_control_2.justification: Network Segmentation (macrosegementation) (NET-06) provides network-level access restriction that compensates for the absence of Use of Demilitarized Zones (DMZ) (WEB-02) by limiting attacker reach and lateral movement opportunities across the environment. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
